claim: Strands agent framework is an open-source SDK that requires only three core components to build agents: models, tools, and prompts
actionable: Use Strands SDK (pip install strand-agents) and provide a system prompt, list of tools, and model to quickly build autonomous agents without heavy scaffolding
source_title: Building AI agents with Claude in Amazon Bedrock | Code w/ Claude

claim: Strands has built-in support for MCP servers and AWS official MCP servers (documentation lookup, diagrams, cost analysis, etc.) enabling agents to access external context
actionable: Connect Strands agents to MCP servers using uvx command to download MCP server and pass it to agent configuration for extended capabilities
source_title: Building AI agents with Claude in Amazon Bedrock | Code w/ Claude

claim: Strands comes with built-in tools like HTTP request, eliminating need to write basic tool handling code and allowing models to decide how to format API calls
actionable: Leverage built-in HTTP request tool in Strands to make API calls without manual tool implementation; let the model determine how to format requests based on system prompt
source_title: Building AI agents with Claude in Amazon Bedrock | Code w/ Claude

claim: Tool decorator in Strands framework is simple: just add @tool decorator above a function definition, no complex boilerplate needed
actionable: Define custom tools in Strands by adding @tool decorator above a function and returning the desired output; framework handles the rest
source_title: Building AI agents with Claude in Amazon Bedrock | Code w/ Claude

claim: Modern LLMs (like Claude 3.5+) are capable enough that agents can be given high flexibility in tool selection and reasoning rather than being constrained by rigid scaffolding
actionable: Design agents with minimal constraints on model reasoning; let the model determine tool usage based on system prompt rather than hard-coded workflows
source_title: Building AI agents with Claude in Amazon Bedrock | Code w/ Claude

claim: Claude Code can be used without API keys when run on Amazon Bedrock through environment variable export, enabling browser-based development with cloud auth
actionable: Set BEDROCK_REGION environment variable to use Claude Code through Bedrock instead of Anthropic API, enabling integrated development in browser-based VS Code
source_title: Building AI agents with Claude in Amazon Bedrock | Code w/ Claude
